Military unit

The **82nd Regiment of Foot (Prince of Wales's Volunteers)** was an infantry [regiment](/wiki/Regiment "Regiment") of the [British Army](/wiki/British_Army "British Army"), raised in 1793. Under the [Childers Reforms](/wiki/Childers_Reforms "Childers Reforms") it amalgamated with the [40th (the 2nd Somersetshire) Regiment of Foot](/wiki/40th_(the_2nd_Somersetshire)_Regiment_of_Foot "40th (the 2nd Somersetshire) Regiment of Foot") to form the [Prince of Wales's Volunteers (South Lancashire Regiment)](/wiki/South_Lancashire_Regiment "South Lancashire Regiment") in 1881.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=82nd_Regiment_of_Foot_(Prince_of_Wales%27s_Volunteers)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

### Formation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=82nd_Regiment_of_Foot_(Prince_of_Wales%27s_Volunteers)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Formation")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/fb/Henry_Pigot.jpg/250px-Henry_Pigot.jpg)](/wiki/File:Henry_Pigot.jpg)

General [Henry Pigot](/wiki/Henry_Pigot "Henry Pigot"), colonel of the regiment throughout the Napoleonic Wars

The regiment was raised by General [Charles Leigh](/wiki/Charles_Leigh_(British_Army_officer) "Charles Leigh (British Army officer)") as the **82nd Regiment of Foot**, in response to the threat posed by the [French Revolution](/wiki/French_Revolution "French Revolution"), on 27 September 1793.[[1]](#cite_note-regt-1) It embarked for the [West Indies](/wiki/West_Indies "West Indies") in June 1795 and was deployed to [Santo Domingo](/wiki/Santo_Domingo "Santo Domingo") in the [Dominican Republic](/wiki/Dominican_Republic "Dominican Republic") in August 1795.[[2]](#cite_note-2) On arrival, the regiment was sent to [Port-au-Prince](/wiki/Port-au-Prince "Port-au-Prince") to reinforce the garrison there.[[3]](#cite_note-jarvis5-3) Over the following year they repelled several attacks from French troops before returning to England in January 1799.[[4]](#cite_note-4) The regiment also took part in an expedition to [Quiberon Bay](/wiki/Quiberon_Bay "Quiberon Bay") in June 1800 and then transferred to [Menorca](/wiki/Menorca "Menorca") in July 1800 before returning home in June 1802.[[5]](#cite_note-5) It absorbed the Prince of Wales's Volunteers in 1802, incorporating their name to become the **82nd Regiment of Foot (Prince of Wales's Volunteers)**.[[1]](#cite_note-regt-1)

### Napoleonic Wars

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=82nd_Regiment_of_Foot_(Prince_of_Wales%27s_Volunteers)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Napoleonic Wars")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/1a/TarifaArco.JPG/250px-TarifaArco.JPG)](/wiki/File:TarifaArco.JPG)

Puerta de Jerez, a [Tarifa](/wiki/Tarifa "Tarifa") city gate from the Middle Ages

A second battalion was raised in August 1804 but remained in the United Kingdom throughout the [Napoleonic Wars](/wiki/Napoleonic_Wars "Napoleonic Wars").[[6]](#cite_note-6) The 1st battalion saw action at the [Battle of Copenhagen](/wiki/Battle_of_Copenhagen_(1807) "Battle of Copenhagen (1807)") in August 1807 during the [Gunboat War](/wiki/Gunboat_War "Gunboat War").[[7]](#cite_note-7) It embarked for Portugal in early August 1808 for service in the [Peninsular War](/wiki/Peninsular_War "Peninsular War")[[8]](#cite_note-8) and saw action at the [Battle of Roliça](/wiki/Battle_of_Roli%C3%A7a "Battle of Roliça") in August 1808,[[9]](#cite_note-9) the [Battle of Vimeiro](/wiki/Battle_of_Vimeiro "Battle of Vimeiro") later that month[[10]](#cite_note-10) and the [Battle of Corunna](/wiki/Battle_of_Corunna "Battle of Corunna") in January 1809 as well as the subsequent evacuation.[[11]](#cite_note-11) The battalion then took part in the disastrous [Walcheren Campaign](/wiki/Walcheren_Campaign "Walcheren Campaign") in autumn 1809.[[12]](#cite_note-12)

The battalion returned to the Peninsula in spring 1811 and saw action at the [Battle of Barrosa](/wiki/Battle_of_Barrosa "Battle of Barrosa") in March 1811,[[13]](#cite_note-13) the [Siege of Tarifa](/wiki/Siege_of_Tarifa_(1812) "Siege of Tarifa (1812)") in December 1811[[14]](#cite_note-14) and the [Battle of Vitoria](/wiki/Battle_of_Vitoria "Battle of Vitoria") in June 1813[[15]](#cite_note-15) as well as the [Siege of San Sebastián](/wiki/Siege_of_San_Sebasti%C3%A1n "Siege of San Sebastián") in July 1813.[[16]](#cite_note-16) It then pursued the French Army into France and fought at the [Battle of the Pyrenees](/wiki/Battle_of_the_Pyrenees "Battle of the Pyrenees") in July 1813,[[17]](#cite_note-17) the [Battle of Nivelle](/wiki/Battle_of_Nivelle "Battle of Nivelle") in November 1813[[18]](#cite_note-18) and the [Battle of the Nive](/wiki/Battle_of_the_Nive "Battle of the Nive") in December 1813[[19]](#cite_note-19) as well as the [Battle of Orthez](/wiki/Battle_of_Orthez "Battle of Orthez") in February 1814.[[20]](#cite_note-20)

The battalion then embarked for North America for service in the [War of 1812](/wiki/War_of_1812 "War of 1812").[[21]](#cite_note-jarvis59-21) It saw action on the Canadian frontier in 1814 which later earned the battle honour 'Niagara'. [[22]](#cite_note-jarvis64-22) The battalion left for home in June 1815 but, shortly after arriving in England, it embarked for [Ostend](/wiki/Ostend "Ostend") from where it marched to Paris.[[23]](#cite_note-jarvis61-23) Meanwhile, the second battalion was disbanded in December 1815.[[24]](#cite_note-24) On the return journey from France, in January 1816, the brig *Boadicea* was [wrecked](/wiki/Wrecking_of_the_Sea_Horse,_Boadicea_and_Lord_Melville "Wrecking of the Sea Horse, Boadicea and Lord Melville") in heavy storms off the east coast of Ireland while carrying 290 troops and 34 women and children from the regiment: 190 people died when the ship went down off [Courtmacsherry](/wiki/Courtmacsherry "Courtmacsherry").[[25]](#cite_note-25)

### The Victorian era

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=82nd_Regiment_of_Foot_(Prince_of_Wales%27s_Volunteers)&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: The Victorian era")]

The regiment embarked for [Mauritius](/wiki/Mauritius "Mauritius") in January 1819[[26]](#cite_note-26) and returned home in March 1832.[[27]](#cite_note-27) It then sailed for [Gibraltar](/wiki/Gibraltar "Gibraltar") in January 1837,[[28]](#cite_note-jarvis72-28) on to the West Indies in December 1839[[28]](#cite_note-jarvis72-28) and on to Canada in April 1843[[29]](#cite_note-jarvis73-29) before returning home in May 1848.[[29]](#cite_note-jarvis73-29) It then embarked for the [Ionian Islands](/wiki/Ionian_Islands "Ionian Islands") in January 1855;[[30]](#cite_note-jarvis77-30) it also saw action at the [Siege of Sevastopol](/wiki/Siege_of_Sevastopol_(1854%E2%80%931855) "Siege of Sevastopol (1854–1855)") in summer 1855 during the [Crimean War](/wiki/Crimean_War "Crimean War").[[30]](#cite_note-jarvis77-30)

The regiment returned to India in February 1857 to help suppress the [Indian Rebellion](/wiki/Indian_Rebellion_of_1857 "Indian Rebellion of 1857").[[31]](#cite_note-31) It took part in the [recapture of Cawnpore](/wiki/Siege_of_Cawnpore "Siege of Cawnpore") in July 1857[[32]](#cite_note-32) and then took part in the [reinforcement of Lucknow](/wiki/Siege_of_Lucknow "Siege of Lucknow") defending the residency until it was relieved in November 1857.[[33]](#cite_note-33) It transferred to [Aden](/wiki/Aden "Aden") in 1869 and returned home in 1870.[[34]](#cite_note-locations-34)

As part of the [Cardwell Reforms](/wiki/Cardwell_Reforms "Cardwell Reforms") of the 1870s, where single-battalion regiments were linked together to share a single depot and recruiting district in the United Kingdom, the 82nd was linked with the [40th (the 2nd Somersetshire) Regiment of Foot](/wiki/40th_(the_2nd_Somersetshire)_Regiment_of_Foot "40th (the 2nd Somersetshire) Regiment of Foot"), and assigned to district no. 14 at [Peninsula Barracks, Warrington](/wiki/Peninsula_Barracks,_Warrington "Peninsula Barracks, Warrington").[[35]](#cite_note-training-35) On 1 July 1881 the [Childers Reforms](/wiki/Childers_Reforms "Childers Reforms") came into effect and the regiment amalgamated with the 40th (the 2nd Somersetshire) Regiment of Foot to form the [Prince of Wales's Volunteers (South Lancashire Regiment)](/wiki/South_Lancashire_Regiment "South Lancashire Regiment").[[1]](#cite_note-regt-1)

## Battle honours

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=82nd_Regiment_of_Foot_(Prince_of_Wales%27s_Volunteers)&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Battle honours")]

Battle honours won by the Regiment were:[[1]](#cite_note-regt-1)

* *Peninsular War*: [Roliça](/wiki/Battle_of_Roli%C3%A7a "Battle of Roliça"), [Vimiera](/wiki/Battle_of_Vimiero "Battle of Vimiero"), [Vitoria](/wiki/Battle_of_Vitoria "Battle of Vitoria"), [Pyrenees](/wiki/Battle_of_the_Pyrenees "Battle of the Pyrenees"), [Nivelle](/wiki/Battle_of_Nivelle "Battle of Nivelle"), [Orthez](/wiki/Battle_of_Orthez "Battle of Orthez"), Peninsula
* *American War of 1812*: [Niagara](/wiki/Battle_of_Lundy%27s_Lane "Battle of Lundy's Lane")
* *Crimean War*: [Sevastopol](/wiki/Siege_of_Sevastopol_(1854%E2%80%931855) "Siege of Sevastopol (1854–1855)")
* *Indian Mutiny*: [Lucknow](/wiki/Siege_of_Lucknow "Siege of Lucknow")

## Colonels of the Regiment

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=82nd_Regiment_of_Foot_(Prince_of_Wales%27s_Volunteers)&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Colonels of the Regiment")]

Regimental colonels were:[[1]](#cite_note-regt-1)

82nd Regiment of Foot

* 1793–1797: Gen. [Charles Leigh](/wiki/Charles_Leigh_(British_Army_officer) "Charles Leigh (British Army officer)")
* 1797–1798: Gen. [James Stuart](/wiki/James_Stuart_(British_Army_officer,_born_1741) "James Stuart (British Army officer, born 1741)")
* 1798–1836: Gen. Sir [Henry Pigot](/wiki/Henry_Pigot "Henry Pigot"), GCMG

82nd Regiment of Foot (Prince of Wales's Volunteers) - (1802)

* 1836–1841: Gen. Sir [John Wilson](/wiki/John_Wilson_(governor) "John Wilson (governor)"), KCB
* 1841–1850: Lt-Gen. Sir [Andrew Pilkington](/wiki/Andrew_Pilkington "Andrew Pilkington"), KCB
* 1850–1856: Lt-Gen. Francis Miles Milman
* 1856–1859: Lt-Gen. Nicholas Hamilton, KH
* 1859–1872: Gen. Hon. [Thomas Ashburnham](/wiki/Thomas_Ashburnham_(general) "Thomas Ashburnham (general)"), CB
* 1872–1881: Gen. [William Samuel Newton](/w/index.php?title=William_Samuel_Newton&action=edit&redlink=1 "William Samuel Newton (page does not exist)")
